title: Amphiglottis corymbosa
taxonomy: Amphiglottis corymbosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Achariaceae. This family is part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is within the class Magnoliopsida, also known as dicotyledonous plants. This plant is native to tropical regions, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants. Further classification details may be found within the broader plant taxonomy.
uses: Amphiglottis corymbosa is used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of the plant's medicinal uses.
